Overview Request flow HTTP API Storage Roadmap

Storage

Sites are plain directories on disk. No database. The whole storage layer is <200 lines of Go in internal/storage.

Where files live

Paths are resolved via github.com/adrg/xdg, which follows the XDG Base Directory Specification on Linux and the platform-native equivalent on macOS.

PurposeLinux (XDG)macOS
Config (token, port)~/.config/crate/config.yaml~/Library/Application Support/crate/config.yaml
Sites~/.local/share/crate/sites/~/Library/Application Support/crate/sites/
Logs~/.local/state/crate/log/~/Library/Application Support/crate/log/
macOS note. adrg/xdg defers to ~/Library/Application Support on macOS by default. If you'd rather have pure XDG layout on macOS too, set XDG_DATA_HOME=$HOME/.local/share (and friends) in your shell and the library will respect it.

Site layout

~/Library/Application Support/crate/sites/
<name>/
    index.html
    style.css
    sub/
        page.html

# transient (only present mid-upload):
.<name>.stage-7xq2/   — new copy being written
.<name>.old-17181...  — previous copy, deleted on success

Atomic replacement

A crate push must not leave a half-replaced site if anything fails — a partial overwrite breaks every reader. The replacement is a three-step rename:

extract tar → sites/.foo.stage-7xq2/
↓ if extraction fails, rm stage and bail — foo/ untouched
rename sites/foo → sites/.foo.old-1718...
↓
rename sites/.foo.stage-7xq2 → sites/foo
↓ if rename fails, rename old back in place
rm sites/.foo.old-1718...

Both renames are within the same directory, so they're atomic on every POSIX filesystem. The window where neither foo/ nor anything-named-foo exists is bounded by one syscall.

Name validation

Site names must match:

^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._-]{0,62}$

That's:

  • Lowercase letter or digit first (no leading dot or hyphen).
  • 1–63 characters total.
  • Only letters, digits, dot, hyphen, underscore.

The pattern keeps names URL-safe, filesystem-safe on every OS, and avoids surprises like a site called .git or ../etc.

Tar-path safety

Every tar entry's path is cleaned and checked before any file is opened:

clean := filepath.Clean(hdr.Name)
if strings.HasPrefix(clean, "..") ||
   strings.HasPrefix(clean, "/")  ||
   strings.Contains(clean, "/../") {
    return ErrUnsafePath
}

Symlinks and hardlinks are silently skipped — they can't escape a sandbox that doesn't honor them. Devices, FIFOs, and other special files are also skipped.
